LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHEVY CHASE BANK, F.S.B. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, FIFTH, SEVENTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. ON DEMURRER, THE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS ARE ACCEPTED AS TRUE. THE ARGUMENTS OF TRUE BENEFICIARY, THE TENDER RULE, AND NO DUTY SOUNDING IN NEGLIGENCE MUST BE RAISED AS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS MUST ALLEGE FRAUD WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (COUNSEL WAIVES NOTICE OF TODAY'S RULING.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHEVY CHASE BANK, F.S.B. DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Oxana Kozlov, Esq. IBCI Law Group, (650) 814-7708 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; James J. Jirn, Archer Norris (925) 930-6600, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. This tentative ruling is not being contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Chevy Chase's demurrer to the fourth and sixth causes of action is sustained with leave to amend to give the plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege fraud with particularity. Plaintiffs are granted until 12/09/09 to amend. Order signed in open Court.(302/HEK)JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR # 7235

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint;
Off Calendar Sep-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHEVY CHASE BANK, F.S.B. Demurrer To First Amended Complaint. Oxana Kozlov, Esq. of IBCI Law Group, (650) 814-7708, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Juliet R. Jonas, of Archer Norris (925) 930-6600, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first, second, fifth, seventh, and eighth causes of action. On demurrer, the factual allegations are accepted as true. The arguments of true beneficiary, the tender rule, and no duty sounding in negligence must be raised as affirmative defenses. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to sixth cause of action. Plaintiffs must allege fraud with greater specificity. Order signed in open Court. (Counsel waives notice of today's ruling.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
